title: Helios — N-Body Studio
description: A real-time gravitational N-body studio: Barnes-Hut O(n log n) solver, SEVEN integrators including 4th- AND 6th-order Yoshida symplectic schemes (Yoshida 6 holds energy to ~1e-13, ~40,000× flatter than Yoshida 4 — near machine precision). A from-scratch CHAOS LAB integrates the variational (tangent) equations with the exact analytic tidal tensor to compute the maximal Lyapunov exponent (Benettin renormalisation) and the MEGNO indicator ⟨Y⟩ (Cincotta & Simó) — telling a regular orbit (⟨Y⟩→2) from a chaotic one (⟨Y⟩ grows linearly) live, with order/chaos classification and MEGNO/λ-vs-time plots. Plus an osculating-orbit overlay that reconstructs the live Kepler ellipse of any body from its eccentricity vector, a restricted-three-body layer drawing the five Lagrange points and the zero-velocity (Hill-region) curves of the Jacobi integral, inelastic collisions & accretion, a shadow-copy orbit forecaster, live energy/momentum/virial diagnostics, fifteen astrophysical presets (the figure-eight choreography and its chaotic 'broken eight' twin, the Pythagorean three-body problem, Saturn's rings, Jupiter's Trojans, an eccentric Kepler showcase, co-orbital horseshoes & tadpoles, a hierarchical triple), colour-by speed/mass/acceleration, shareable permalinks, and an in-app 15-check numerical self-test that re-derives every physical claim live — down to a finite-difference proof of the tidal tensor and the time-reversibility of the integrator — all hand-written TypeScript on a Canvas.
